checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?
checks.guidance: Cross-check the result against explicit user notes, critique corrections, and watch items. Flag anything the implementation ignored, contradicted, or only partially covered.

findings.detail: Checked the user’s explicit markdown-rendering scope addition against v3 and the repo. That part is still correctly covered: the plan keeps the shared `AssetDescription` change for both `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:166-169` and `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx:186-189`, which matches the note and the actual plain-text call sites.

The plan still intentionally diverges from the approved runtime shape, and that remains a flag. The brief asked for two Supabase Edge Functions, a promoter directory under `supabase/functions/`, and a resumable cursor table; v3 still replaces the promoter with PL/pgSQL and removes the cursor table entirely. Documenting the deviation is useful, but it does not make it cease to be a deviation from the approved deliverables.

The revision also drops the requested first-working-version milestone of 'schema + promoter dry-run' before media rehosting. The brief explicitly calls for promoter dry-run behavior, and the repo has multiple existing dry-run patterns in scripts, but v3 has no promoter preview mode at all and jumps straight to live `SELECT internal.discord_promote_resources()` validation.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the user’s markdown-rendering scope addition against the revised plan and the repo. This part is now explicitly covered: the plan adds a shared `AssetDescription` component and says it will replace the plain-text description blocks in both `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:166-169` and `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx:186-189`, which matches the user note and closes the earlier gap.

The revision diverges from the user’s stated runtime/deliverable shape in a way that still needs to be flagged. The brief asked for two Supabase Edge Functions plus pg_cron, with a promoter function directory under `supabase/functions/`, and a resumable per-channel cursor table; v2 replaces the promoter with PL/pgSQL and removes the cursor table entirely. That may be a pragmatic simplification, but it is still a requirements change rather than a straight implementation of the approved shape.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?
checks.guidance: Look for logic errors, invalid assumptions, broken invariants, schema mismatches, or behavior that would fail at runtime. When the fix adds a conditional branch, check whether it handles all relevant cases — not just the one reported in the issue.

findings.detail: Checked the revised resource type mapping against the website code and that part is still correct. `banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13` only supports `lora | workflow`, and v3 keeps the `resources -> workflow` / other four channels -> `lora` mapping that matches the repo.

The main promoter insert still does not implement the anti-join behavior the overview claims. In Step 7, the valid asset insert selects every qualifying OP with an existing member and relies on `ON CONFLICT (discord_thread_id) DO UPDATE`; because there is no `NOT EXISTS` filter on the insert path, every rerun will re-update already imported assets and `GET DIAGNOSTICS v_a = ROW_COUNT` will not stay at zero, which conflicts with the plan’s own idempotency/counting claims.

The observability SQL in the plan is still mismatched to the actual schema. `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` defines `system_logs` with `logger_name` and `extra` columns, not `component` and `payload`, yet v3 still proposes inserts like `INSERT INTO public.system_logs (component, level, message, payload) ...`. As written, those logging statements would fail until the column names are corrected.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the revised type mapping against the repo and that specific defect is fixed. The plan now maps Discord resource posts to `workflow` or `lora`, which matches the website’s real resource enum in `banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13` and the current filter/card branches that key off `workflow`.

The replacement for FLAG-001 is still incomplete. V2 no longer uses `created_at > cursor`, but it still limits discovery to `discord_messages.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'`; a thread created 61+ days ago that only later reaches 5 reactions would still never import, even though the product rule says any qualifying OP becomes an asset and the ongoing sync is not scoped to recent posts only.

The plan’s failure-handling story for missing members is not technically sufficient in SQL form. It says an FK miss will be 'logged and skipped', but the proposed promoter logic is a set-based `INSERT ... SELECT` inside one PL/pgSQL function; if one row violates the `members(member_id)` FK, PostgreSQL aborts the statement unless the plan introduces row-level exception isolation, so one bad author can block the whole promoter run rather than just one asset/comment.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?
checks.guidance: Look at how the changed function is used across the codebase. Does the fix only address one caller's scenario while others remain broken? Flag missing required work or out-of-scope edits. A minimal patch is often right, but check whether the underlying problem is bigger than what the issue describes.

findings.detail: Checked the earlier UI-surface problem against the v3 simplification and that particular scope gap is resolved. `ResourceDetail` still has only inline `activeMedia` state at `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:59-74`, so downgrading comment/media interaction to ordinary `<a target="_blank">` links now fits the real surface instead of inventing an unsupported lightbox boundary.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the prior surface-scope problems against the revised repo references and those specific fixes are in place at the plan level. V2 moves the primary UI work to `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx`, which is the actual routed surface, and it explicitly threads `is_hidden` through `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, and `useUserProfile` instead of leaving the kill switch modal-local.

The discussion/media interaction work is still broader than the plan acknowledges. `ResourceDetail` currently only swaps `activeMedia` inline (`banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:59-74, 228-245`) and the resource area has no existing comment lightbox component to reuse; the plan nevertheless says comment thumbnails will open an 'existing lightbox' anchored to the parent comment. That means a non-trivial new modal/state/navigation surface is still missing from the scoped implementation steps.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?
checks.guidance: Search for all instances of the symbol/pattern being changed. Also ask: does this feature require setup, registration, or integration code beyond the core logic? Missing glue code causes test failures even when the core fix is correct.

findings.detail: Checked the proposed storage bucket/path against the shared Supabase setup and that part lines up. The shared workspace already creates a public `user-uploads` bucket in `supabase/migrations/20260326000000_consolidate_workspace.sql:39-75`, and the website already writes to `user-uploads` from places like `banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitArt/index.tsx:62-73`.

The new all-history promoter strategy is still missing supporting index work. `supabase/migrations/20251101000000_create_discord_tables.sql:29-36` gives `discord_messages` only single-column indexes on `channel_id`, `created_at`, `author_id`, `reference_id`, `synced_at`, `thread_id`, and `is_deleted`; there is no composite or partial index backing the new every-10-min scan of `channel_id` plus `message_id = thread_id` plus `reaction_count >= 5` plus `is_deleted = FALSE`. v3 calls that scan 'cheap' and 'trivially small', but the repository does not currently provide the index support for that claim.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the previously missing glue paths and that part of the revision is covered. The plan now explicitly updates `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, and `useUserProfile`, so the earlier missing `source`/`discord_channel_id`/`is_hidden` propagation problem is addressed at the plan level.

The Cloudflare reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository code. `supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` only writes `cloudflare_playback_hls_url`, `cloudflare_thumbnail_url`, and `storage_provider`; it does not persist duration, even though the revised plan and success criteria say the existing webhook will populate HLS, thumbnail, and duration for imported videos. That supporting-infrastructure contract is still inaccurate unless the plan adds a webhook change or drops duration from the promise.

The new vault bootstrap step is not yet self-contained enough to satisfy the plan’s own `supabase db reset` criterion. There is no existing `vault.create_secret`, `internal.get_service_role_key`, or secret-seeding pattern anywhere under `supabase/`, and the proposed migration depends on an out-of-band `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key_value', true)` value that the repo never sets. As written, the bootstrap is still an infrastructure gap rather than a ready migration recipe.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?
checks.guidance: Grep for call sites. For each caller, check what values it passes — especially edge cases like None, zero, empty, or composite inputs. Then ask: should this change be here, or in a caller, callee, or new method?

findings.detail: Checked the concrete caller path again and the revised interaction model now matches it. `ResourceDetail` only owns `activeMedia` switching and `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` still finds no live callers for the modal, so the new-tab anchor approach no longer depends on any nonexistent caller boundary or modal state.

I also re-checked the data-loading callers that feed these surfaces. The four hooks identified in earlier iterations remain the right fan-out points for `source`, `discord_channel_id`, and `is_hidden`, so v3’s choice to thread the new fields through `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, and `useUserProfile` is consistent with the real caller graph.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the prior caller propagation defect against the revised plan and that part is addressed. The plan now explicitly revises the four existing resource-consuming hooks (`useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile`) so the real callers that feed cards, detail pages, and profile counts would at least receive `source`, `discord_channel_id`, and `is_hidden`.

I also checked the concrete caller that would need to host the new interaction model. `ResourceDetail` currently has only `activeMedia`/`setActiveMedia` state and no comment-modal or lightbox caller boundary (`banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:59-74`), while `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` still shows no imports/usages for the modal. The plan’s promise of an 'existing lightbox' anchored to a parent comment therefore still lacks a real caller path in the current codebase.

flags.concern: Discord resource sync planning: v3 still diverges from the approved deliverable shape and drops the requested promoter dry-run / first-working-version milestone.
flags.evidence: The brief explicitly asks for two Supabase Edge Functions, a promoter directory under `supabase/functions/`, a resumable cursor table, and a first milestone of `schema + promoter dry-run`; v3 keeps the PL/pgSQL promoter/no-cursor pivot and has no promoter dry-run path.

flags.concern: Discord sync logging: the revised plan still targets nonexistent `system_logs` columns, so the proposed promoter/importer logging SQL would fail against the actual schema.
flags.evidence: `supabase/migrations/20251210000000_create_system_logs.sql:5-18` defines `logger_name` and `extra`, not `component` and `payload`, while v3 Step 7 and Step 23 still use `component`/`payload` in example inserts.

flags.concern: Discord promoter idempotency: the revised main insert path still lacks the claimed anti-join, so reruns will update every already-imported qualifying OP and its row-count/idempotency claims will not hold as written.
flags.evidence: In v3 Step 7, the valid asset insert filters only on channel, OP/thread identity, reaction threshold, deletion state, and member existence, then uses `ON CONFLICT (discord_thread_id) DO UPDATE`; it does not include `NOT EXISTS` on `assets.discord_thread_id` for the main insert path.

flags.concern: Discord promoter performance: the new all-history OP scan is not backed by a matching composite or partial index in `discord_messages`.
flags.evidence: `supabase/migrations/20251101000000_create_discord_tables.sql:29-36` only creates single-column indexes on `discord_messages`; there is no index tailored to `channel_id` + OP rows + `reaction_count >= 5` + `is_deleted = FALSE`, even though v3 removes the time window and runs every 10 minutes.
